title: Kubernetes (DevOps view)
objectives: Roll out safely with health checks + progressive delivery

sections.markdown: For DevOps, Kubernetes is your **container orchestrator + rollout engine**. You declare desired state in YAML/Helm; controllers converge, do rolling updates, restart bad pods, and autoscale — but only if you set the right probes and resource requests.

Modern K8s stack = **GitOps**. The desired state of the cluster lives in git; Argo CD / Flux continuously reconcile. Deployments become PRs — reviewable, auditable, and easy to roll back by reverting the commit.

Missing readiness probes = 500 storms during rollouts. Missing PodDisruptionBudget = evictions take out too many replicas. Missing resource requests = CPU-starved pods + noisy neighbors. Fix the trio before shipping anything to prod.

Progressive delivery with canary (5% → 25% → 100%) tied to SLO checks (error rate + latency) auto-rolls back on regression. Combined with GitOps + immutable digests, deploys become boring — which is the goal.

flashcards.back: Cluster state is a function of a git repo; Argo CD / Flux continuously converge cluster to repo.
Canary in stages (5→25→100%) with automatic rollback on SLO regression.

quiz.q: Under GitOps the cluster state is…

quiz.explain: Traffic goes to pods that aren't ready.

options: Argo Rollouts / Flagger canary tied to SLOs

challenges.text: Deploy an app to kind with Deployment + Service + probes.
Templatize with Kustomize; two overlays (dev, prod).
Deploy via Argo CD from a git repo; test rollback via revert.
Add Argo Rollouts canary tied to SLO checks (error rate + latency).

interviewQuestions: What is GitOps and why use it?
Progressive delivery vs plain rolling update?
How do you handle secrets in K8s + git?
How do you monitor deployment health?
